title: Promotion List Recommendation
recommendation: Remove Employee L-1004 from the promotion shortlist due to low predicted leadership fit.
hidden_risk: The recommendation affects employment outcomes, is weakly grounded, and may encode bias. The AI has not cited enough role-relevant evidence or provided an appeal path.
policy_refs: HR-AI-Policy §2.1: High-impact employment decisions require human review.
Responsible AI Guide §4.3: Automated recommendations must include evidence and appeal path.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should not let an AI system make or quietly influence high-impact HR decisions without policy-backed evidence, human review, and an appeal route.

title: Refund Policy Automation
recommendation: Automatically deny the customer refund because their case appears outside the return window.
hidden_risk: The retrieved policy is stale. The policy changed last quarter for warranty exceptions, so the AI may deny a valid claim.
policy_refs: Support Runbook §3.2: Refund denials require current policy citation.
Grounding Standard §1.4: Stale retrieval must be challenged before action.
supervisor_lesson: When an AI cites a policy, the supervisor must check whether the policy is current and relevant before approving customer-impacting automation.

title: Vendor Auto-Approval
recommendation: Approve Vendor V-204 immediately because the quote is 18% cheaper than alternatives.
hidden_risk: The agent ignored missing compliance documentation and over-optimised for cost.
policy_refs: Procurement Policy §5.1: Vendors cannot be approved without compliance documents.
AI Tool Use Policy §2.7: Cost-saving recommendations must not bypass governance controls.
supervisor_lesson: Cost savings are not enough to approve a vendor. The AI must satisfy governance and compliance requirements before action is allowed.
